paper_title: Palaeomagnetic and Rock Magnetic Analysis of Rose Cottage Cave Sediments

methods.description: Process for collecting palaeomagnetic samples from sediment layers in Rose Cottage Cave to study past magnetic fields.

steps.description: The palaeomagnetic samples (n=92) were taken from the fine sediments of the southern profile exposed by Wadley's excavations.
steps.searchable_summary: collect palaeomagnetic samples from sediment layers

steps.description: The samples were vertically taken after carefully cleaning the section, then trying to follow the apparently undisturbed layers suitable for our research.
steps.searchable_summary: clean and vertically collect samples from undisturbed layers

steps.description: The cores were taken using 25-mm long and 20-mm diameter cylindrical PVC plastic containers.
steps.searchable_summary: use PVC containers to collect sediment cores

steps.description: The sample's orientation was measured using a Brunton compass.
steps.searchable_summary: measure sample orientation with Brunton compass

steps.description: Samples were consolidated with sodium silicate once removed and they were numbered from the top to the bottom.
steps.searchable_summary: consolidate samples with sodium silicate and number them

methods.description: Analysis of magnetic properties of sediment samples to differentiate between burned and unburned sediments and to characterize magnetic mineralogy.

steps.description: In our studies we used a Kappabridge AGICO of three frequencies (1000 Hz, 4000 Hz and 16 000 Hz) and we also measured the dependence of the susceptibility at high (room temperature to near 700 °C) and low (room temperature to near -190 °C) temperatures.
steps.searchable_summary: measure magnetic susceptibility at various frequencies and temperatures

steps.description: The variation of initial magnetic susceptibility with frequency was used as an indicator of superparamagnetic (SP) magnetite grains.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze frequency dependence of magnetic susceptibility for SP magnetite

steps.description: Another parameter we used was proposed by Hrouda as X R = (X f1 -X f2 )/ (X f2 -X f3 ).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Hrouda's parameter for SP grain size estimation

steps.description: Typical magnetic susceptibility versus temperature curves are depicted in Figure 3b.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze magnetic susceptibility vs temperature curves

steps.description: The slope is related to the stable single domain/superparamagnetic transition because the blocking volume is proportional to temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze slope for domain transition in magnetic grains
